title: Action Plan: Edinburgh Historical Weather via NOAA datasets (Filesystem JSON first)
goal: Build a research-grade NOAA ingestion path for Edinburgh historical weather, using station-based records where available, and persist normalized hourly JSON outputs to filesystem.

must_be_confirmed_before_build: Exact NOAA dataset/API selection finalized (for example CDO API vs bulk datasets).
Token requirements confirmed for selected NOAA path and token provisioned if needed.
Station selection strategy for Edinburgh approved (primary + fallback list policy).
Historical date range and acceptable sparsity/gap policy approved.
Variable mapping and normalization rules approved.
Filesystem path, schedule, and operational ownership confirmed.
credentials_and_access: Some NOAA APIs require tokens (for example CDO token); verify requirements up front.
If using bulk/public files, verify download bandwidth and storage constraints.

included: NOAA station discovery strategy for Edinburgh region
Historical data pull and wrangling pipeline
Normalization into canonical hourly JSON files
Basic quality flags and missing-data handling
excluded: Global station rollout beyond Edinburgh area

phases.name: Upfront Requirements Gate
tasks: Finalize dataset/API path and credential model before implementation.
Run station discovery dry run for Edinburgh and document chosen station set.
Capture real sample payloads for parser design and edge-case planning.
Do not start ingestion build until definition_of_ready_checklist is complete.

phases.name: Overview and Scope
tasks: Choose NOAA endpoint/dataset path suitable for UK station history.
Define station-selection policy (primary station plus fallback station list).
Define normalization policy for sparse or irregular intervals.
Define hourly output expectation and documented caveats.

phases.name: Filesystem Data Layer
tasks: Create storage path (for example data/weather/noaa/edinburgh).
Create canonical JSON schema with source station metadata and quality flags.
Create checkpoint manifest with station cursor, date cursor, and retry state.
Use atomic writes and deterministic partitioning (year/month).

phases.name: Backend Integration Layer
tasks: Implement station resolver for Edinburgh coordinates and proximity ranking.
Implement NOAA client wrapper with pagination and retry/backoff.
Implement parser/wrangler to convert raw observations to hourly rows.
Fill/flag gaps explicitly rather than silently interpolating by default.
Persist normalized rows and source metadata snapshots to filesystem.

phases.name: API and UX Layer
tasks: Add trigger endpoint for station resolution + historical ingest.
Add status endpoint with station currently in use and date cursor.
Add read endpoint for hourly JSON outputs with optional source metadata.
Keep UX minimal and transparent about data gaps.

phases.name: Testing
tasks: Unit tests for station ranking and time-bucket normalization.
Unit tests for missing-value and quality-flag handling.
Integration tests with mocked NOAA payloads and real filesystem writes.
E2E test for trigger/status/read path with representative sparse data.

phases.name: Documentation
tasks: Document NOAA source limitations and expected data sparsity.
Document station choice logic and fallback behavior.
Document canonical schema and quality flag semantics.
Document operational guidance for reruns and checkpoint recovery.

phases.name: Deployment and Verification
tasks: Run quality gates and verify reproducible output files.
Validate sample periods against known weather events in Edinburgh.
Verify reruns are idempotent and recover cleanly from interruptions.
Record source comparison notes against Open-Meteo and Met Office outputs.

notes: NOAA plan prioritizes provenance and raw-station traceability.
Wrangling complexity is expected and explicitly scoped.
